summary: The main claim is: If you view alignment as a 'dam' constraining AI power, imperfect methods are nearly useless because you can't reach 'almost perfect' by stacking imperfect ones.

But under a concept-extrapolation / model-splintering view — safely managing the AI's transition across environment changes — imperfect methods can compose and remain genuinely valuable, which has large strategic consequences.
